title: Checking and Adjusting for Proper Focal Distance
description:
Checking and Adjusting for Proper Focal Distance

Checking and Adjusting for Proper Focal Distance

	Position your camera so that it is located
	 above the area that you will be searching for parts and place an object
	 within the camera's field of view.  This will give us a target
	 for adjusting the focus of the camera.
	You should now see, in the image display
	 area of the Vision Guide window, a view of the object that you put
	 under the camera.  If you cannot, try manually picking up the
	 part and moving the target feature closer and further away from the
	 lens.  Do you see the target feature now?  (We are just
	 trying to see the part but it may be way out of focus so don't worry
	 about that yet.)  If you don't then you probably have selected
	 the wrong lens for the distance your part is away from the camera.
	 Try a different sized lens.
	A 25 or 50 mm lens should work for most mobile
	 cameras mounted to the robot.  So if you are trying to test with
	 a Mobile camera mounted to the robot, we suggest a 25 mm lens to start
	 with.
	At this point you can see at least the target
	 feature on the work surface.  Adjust the focus of the lens to
	 bring the part into good focus.  If you cannot bring the part
	 into focus, then you may need to add an extension tube between the
	 lens and the camera.  This will change the focal distance for
	 the lens and with the proper extension tube you should be able to
	 focus the camera.
	After installing an extension tube, try adjusting
	 the focus of the camera lens.  You may have to experiment with
	 different extension tube lengths to get proper focus.

Refer to Extension Tube Work Distance for a list of the common lens and extension tube configurations for various focal lengths.

Now the installation is completed.  Go on to Software 10.1 Quick Start: A Vision Guide 8.0 Tutorial.  This chapter describes how to position the parts and how to carry them by the robot using the Vision Guide.
